title: salix × friesiana nothosubsp. antverpiensis
intro: Salix × friesiana nothosubsp. antverpiensis, also known as the Antwerp willow, is a plant hybrid between Salix alba and Salix fragilis. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe, often found growing in wetlands and riverbanks. The medicinal properties of Salix × friesiana nothosubsp. antverpiensis include its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, thanks to its high concentration of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are said to provide health benefits like reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and soothing skin irritations.
